title: Bacteria Build Cities Called Biofilms
script_summary: Explore how biofilms protect bacteria and challenge antibiotic treatment.

segments.voiceover: Ever heard of cities built by bacteria? They’re called biofilms.
These slimy structures can protect bacteria from our immune system and antibiotics.
Imagine a fortress where bacteria thrive, making infections harder to treat.
Studies show that biofilms can lead to chronic infections, resisting many antibiotics.
Understanding biofilms is key to developing new treatments against stubborn infections.
Curious about more microscopic wonders? Hit that like button and follow for more!
